notes: Spanner PostgreSQL dialect PR (124 files, diff truncated in test-file region; all source files SpannerPostgreSQLDialect/PostgreSQLDialect/SpannerConcatFunction/SpannerTimeJdbcType/AbstractSqlAstTranslator visible). GOOD: (1) contributePostgreSQLTypes override omits super() call so most base JDBC type registrations are dropped - real runtime risk, verified no super call in diff (type list cites base class not in diff but core claim grounded); (2) appendDateTimeLiteral(Calendar) overload missing @Override while sibling TemporalAccessor/Date overloads have it - confirmed; (3) commented-out getCheckCondition(long[]) dead code - confirmed lines present; (5) SpannerConcatFunction CAST="::text" constant unused, literals hardcoded - confirmed; (6) SpannerTimeJdbcType.INSTANCE typed as base TimeJdbcType not own type - confirmed. TRIVIAL: (4) double-space after '=' style nit; (7) createScalarSubquery uses getDialect().getDual() vs getDual() helper - pure style, no defect. MISSED: human-ref DialectFeatureChecks 'like...escape' emulation suggestion (file not in visible window, possibly past truncation, but a real PR point). No fabrications - claims about base-class internals are explicitly framed as base behavior. Solid, grounded review for a test PR; mostly minor findings plus one substantive type-registration issue.
